content.full_text:
State of California                    A-19-059

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration · · · Executive Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 323-5660                                322-6441               322-6444

April 18, 1979


John R. Clausen
Contra Costa County
Counsel's Office
Post Office Box 69
Martinez, CA 94553

Dear Mr. Clausen:

Recently, I spoke with Mr. Bark Gilbert, Deputy Director
of the Department of Public Works for Contra Costa County, with
respect to whether Frank Boerger, a consultant to the Department,
should be designated in the Department's Conflict of Interest
Code. I indicated to Mr. Gilbert that I would write you a
letter regarding the matter.

The facts provided to our office indicate that Mr. Boerger
has been employed by the Department of Public Works and the
County in various consultant capacities since 1969. Currently,
Mr. Boerger is advising the Department regarding various water-
related construction projects within the County including the
San Francisco Bay to Stockton Ship Channel (Baldwin Channel).
In addition to these activities, it is contemplated that
Mr. Boerger, on behalf of the County, will present recommendations
and give testimony before congressional appropriation
committees for federal support of water-related projects within
the County.

The Political Reform Act requires public officials to be
designated in a conflict of interest code if they make or participate in the making of governmental decisions that may materially
affect financial interests. Government Code Section 87300 et seq.
Public official is defined in Government Code Section 82048
to mean every "member, officer, employee, or consultant of a
state or local government agency." (Emphasis added.)

The Commission has clarified the meaning of the term
"consultant" as used in the Political Reform Act in Regulation
18700 which provides:

Mr. John B. Clausen
April 18, 1979
Page Two


     (a) "Public official at any level of state or
local government" means every natural person who is
a member, officer, employee or consultant of a state
or local government agency.

     (2) "Consultant" shall include any natural
person who provides, under contract, information,
advice, recommendation or counsel to a state or
local government agency, provided, however, that
"consultant" shall not include a person who:

          (A) Conducts research and arrives
     conclusions with respect to his or her
     rendition of information, advice, recommen-
     dation or counsel independent of the control
     and direction of the agency or any agency
     official, other than normal contract moni-
     toring; and

          (B) Possesses no authority with
     respect to any agency decision beyond the
     rendition of information, advice, recommendation
     or counsel.

                    2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(2)

     Based on my conversation with Mr. Gilbert, it is my under-
standing that the advice and recommendations provided by Mr. Boerger
do not come within any of the exceptions to the definition of
consultant as set forth in Regulation 18700(a)(2)(A) and (B).
Therefore, if the type of governmental decisions Mr. Boerger
participates in as a consultant for the Department of Public
Works can materially affect financial interests, his position
should be included in the Public Works Department's Conflict
of Interest Code.

     If you have any questions regarding this matter, please do
not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dwight Dickerson
                                        Legal Counsel
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

cc:  Bart Gilbert
     Virginia Rice
     Lee Sanders

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                  Date :April 18, 1979


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Dwight Dickerson

Subject: Virginia Rice's Correspondence Referred to us by Bill Tyndall
         on March 14, 1979


Ms. Rice wanted to know under what circumstances would a
consultant be designated in a Conflict of Interest Code.
Called Ms. Rice on March 26, 1979. I indicated to her what
the Commission's standards were for designating consultants
in Conflict of Interest Codes. I told her that I would call
the Contra Costa County Counsel and discuss with him whether
Mr. Boerger, a consultant in the Public Works Department,
should be designated in the Department's Code.

DED:nrw

FOIA TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE

DATE
CORRESPONDENT [illegible] REC'D. 5-116

DATED 5/12 RE: [illegible]

ADMINISTRATION CONFLICTS ENFORCEMENT
BOB T. FED BOB B.
LINDA DWIGHT ROGER
BEV SARAH BOB G.
ALICE MARTIN
GINA T. JIM

EXECUTIVE
DAN LEGAL TAKA
MICKEY BARBARA JOHN K. JAY
CARLA Z. LEE GINA S.K. RICH
JEANNE NANCY MARYANN NAN-L.A.
BOB S. BERMA HELEN-L.A.
JOHN G.

INFORMATION REQUEST FOR ADVICE--MUST BE ANSWERED WITHIN 21 DA

[illegible] PLEASE:

TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE
REPLY DIRECT BY 5/26 --COPY TO [illegible]
PREPARE SUGGESTED REPLY AND RETURN TO
COORDINATE REPLY WITH

Talked to Dan 5/26

Elizabeth Heavey Dept CC
415-372-2079

Virginia Heck — Lee Sander's
415-2287-8972 462-6250

Vernon Cline
415-372-2105

490 Windsor St 5
Rohnert "CA"
94X.
765-2340

common cause
2964 Fillmore Street
San Francisco, CA 94123 • (415) 567-9799

March 14, 1979

Lee -

Can you take a look at this memo from
one of our best volunteers and evaluate
her contentions.
She is interested in any informal guidance
you can offer.

Thanks

Bu Uydalh

6919 Wilson Way
El Cerrito, CA 94530
March 12, 1979


TO: Lee Rosenthal
    Virginia Rice

FROM: Virginia Rice, West Contra Costa Common Cause, Tel: 237-5972

SUBJECT: Frank Boerger, Contra Costa County Consultant on water-related
         projects

ACTION REQUESTED: Your informal comments on whether under
                  Sections 82048, 87100, 87102 (c), and 87302
                  of the Political Reform Act of 1974, the subject
                  should be covered by the Conflict of Interest Code
                  required of the Contra Costa County Public Works
                  Department and therefore be required to disclose
                  his sources of income.

REFERENCES: For your convenience, Section 82048 gives the definition
            of public official as a member, officer, employee or
            consultant of a state or local government agency. Section
            87100 states that no public official at any level of
            government shall make, participate in making or in any
            way attempt to use his official position to influence a
            governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to
            know he has a financial interest. Section 87102 (c) states
            that a financial interest consists of any source of income
            "aggregating $250 or more in value received by or promised
            to the public official within twelve months prior
            to the time when the decision was made.

Attachment: List of positions covered in the Contra Costa County
            Public Works Department Conflict of Interest Code filed
            with the County Clerk. (Bd. of Sups. Code Reviewing Body)

PROFILE OF SUBJECT: Col. Frank Boerger, U.S. Army Retired; Graduate
                    of Westpoint, 1947; Masters Civil Engineering,
                    University of Illinois; Resident of San Rafael, Marin County, large
                    family, church worker, telephone (415) 472-1400; District Engineer
                    for San Francisco, Army Corps of Engineers, 1966-69. Well-liked,
                    "everybody's water expert".

County Employment (Contra Costa County)

Public Works Department, first hired August 26, 1969.

1969-1974: Miscellaneous jobs                               $2,700
1973-1977: "County water executive" as staff on loan
           to West Contra Costa Waste Water Management
           Agency (CWA) where he was thought of as
           county staff. (Decisions related to growth) $57,100
1969-1979: Consultant, Baldwin Ship Channel Project*      $44,600
(to date) Consultant, County Solid Waste Management
           Board (How paid unknown)
           Consultant, ACCENT (Joint Powers Agency to
           promote Baldwin Ship Channel between Solano
           County, Port of Stockton, Contra County)
                                                    (continued)

-2-

Contra Costa County paid 50% of
his $37,400 salary (or fees)                                    18,700

Total paid to date by county
(as far as can be determined)                              $123,100

February 6, '79  Board of Supervisors voted to increase
his "salary" by $7,500 (4-1 vote)

Possible Conflicts: (with County employment where his job obviously
is to give unbiased information on technical
matters to carry out county policy.)

Col. Boerger is a consultant to: (these are matters of public record)

Wickland Oil Company    (Interested in extension of Baldwin Channel
beyond Richmond.)

Shell Oil Company                    "

Port of Stockton                     "

U.S. Army Corps of Engineers        "

UCO (Urich) Refinery [illegible]     "    (It must get permits from County,
BCDC. Boerger testified on its
behalf before City of Martinez Council

Dow Chemical Plant                   "

Port Costa Brickworks (Walked fill permits through Corps of Engineers)

Benicia Industries                   "

Acme Fill (Dump) (At present applying for county permit to fill an
additional 200 acres.)

(Industrial Tank) (Has two class 1 unauthorized hazardous waste ponds
(related to            on Acme fill border -- no runoff from other water
above)                 quality Board, State Health or County Board)
Boerger is consultant to County Solid Waste Mgt Bd.)

Norcal (Northern California Ports & Terminals Bureau Inc.) He testi-
fied on their behalf at BCDC.

(In addition to the above he works closely with the Port of Richmond
and the Contra Costa County Development Association. He is constantly
in the company of the leaders and directors of both associations.)

(He either is or works for Harding Lawson Associates.)

*The Baldwin Channel is a project to dredge a 45-foot deep channel
from the Golden Gate to Pt. Edith in Suisun Bay, then a 35-foot deep
channel from there to the Port of Stockton, with a possible extension

(continued)

-3-

to Sacramento. County policy is firmly established only for the
section as far as the port of Richmond. The remainder is highly
controversial, involving special interests in the form of
dollars versus damage to the delta, Bay, water supply, marshes, etc.

The Board of Supervisors is under great pressure on this subject
and is very indecisive about how far the Channel should go and is
in need of sound unbiased advice. Currently Col. Rogers is
thought of as a great "bargain" in that he knows everyone in Sacramento and Washington and therefore can "grease" the wheels for
money needed for studies and work. He should be made to disclose
his other income in order for a true evaluation to be made of just
where his real interests lie.

EXHIBIT "A"

Contra Costa County
Public Works Department
Conflict of Interest Code

Director and Consultants who provide legal, financial and purchasing advice

Ex Officio Chief Engineer Contra Costa County Storm Drainage District

sections.conclusion: Based on my conversation with Mr. Gilbert, it is my understanding that the advice and recommendations provided by Mr. Boerger do not come within any of the exceptions to the definition of consultant as set forth in Regulation 18700(a)(2)(A) and (B). Therefore, if the types of governmental decisions Mr. Boerger participates in as a consultant for the Department of Public Works can materially affect financial interests, his position should be included in the Public Works Department's Conflict of Interest Code.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Frank Boerger, a consultant to Contra Costa County's Department of Public Works involved in water-related projects and potentially testifying before congressional committees, should be designated in the Department's Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that Frank Boerger should be designated in the Conflict of Interest Code if his consultant activities involve governmental decisions that can materially affect financial interests, because his work does not qualify for the regulatory exceptions for independent consultants.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes formal advice letter from FPPC (April 18, 1979), supporting internal memo (April 18, 1979), and substantial background documentation including transmittal memo, initial inquiry from Virginia Rice (March 12, 1979), and detailed profile of consultant's activities and potential conflicts. Clear Q&C structure present in main advice letter.

QUESTION: Whether Frank Boerger, a consultant to Contra Costa County's Department of Public Works involved in water-related projects and potentially testifying before congressional committees, should be designated in the Department's Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Based on my conversation with Mr. Gilbert, it is my understanding that the advice and recommendations provided by Mr. Boerger do not come within any of the exceptions to the definition of consultant as set forth in Regulation 18700(a)(2)(A) and (B). Therefore, if the types of governmental decisions Mr. Boerger participates in as a consultant for the Department of Public Works can materially affect financial interests, his position should be included in the Public Works Department's Conflict of Interest Code.

I indicated to Mr. Gilbert that I would write you a letter regarding the matter. The facts provided to our office indicate that Mr. Boerger has been employed by the Department of Public Works and the County in various consultant capacities since 1969. Currently, Mr. Boerger is advising the Department regarding various water-related construction projects within the County including the San Francisco Bay to Stockton Ship Channel (Baldwin Channel). In addition to these activities, it is contemplated that Mr. Boerger, on behalf of the County, will present recommendations and give testimony before congressional appropriation committees for federal support of water-related projects within the County. The Political Reform Act requires public officials to be designated in a conflict of interest code if they make or participate in the making of governmental decisions that may materially affect financial interests. Government Code Section 87300 et seq. Public official is defined in Government Code Section 82048 to mean every "member, officer, employee, or consultant of a state or local government agency." (Emphasis added.) The Commission has clarified the meaning of the term "consultant" as used in the Political Reform Act in Regulation 18700 which provides: (a) "Public official at any level of state or local government" means every natural person who is a member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(2) Based on my conversation with Mr. Gilbert, it is my understanding that the advice and recommendations provided by Mr. Boerger do not come within any of the exceptions to the definition of consultant as set forth in Regulation 18700(a)(2)(A) and (B). Therefore, if the types of governmental decisions Mr. Boerger participates in as a consultant for the Department of Public Works can materially affect financial interests, his position should be included in the Public Works Department's Conflict of Interest Code. If you have any questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me. embedding.summary: FPPC legal counsel advises Contra Costa County that consultant Frank Boerger should be included in the Public Works Department's Conflict of Interest Code if he participates in decisions that materially affect financial interests, as his advisory role does not meet regulatory exceptions for independent consultants.
